{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

revision.text:
{{otheruses|京都市東山区の神社|若宮八幡宮の全般的な説明|若宮八幡宮}} {{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡宮社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|若宮八幡宮]]
参道 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[東山区]]五条橋東五丁目480 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 59 | 緯度秒 = 43.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 29.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-26 | 地図 = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[応神天皇]]
[[仲哀天皇]]
[[神功皇后]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[天喜]]元年（[[1053年]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = 六条左女牛八幡宮・陶器神社 |札所等 = [[洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]]第19番（若宮天満宮） |例祭 = [[8月8日]] |神事 = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|清水焼発祥之地 石碑]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|鍾馗神社 鍾馗像]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|本殿]] '''若宮八幡宮社'''（わかみやはちまんぐうしゃ）は、[[京都市]][[東山区]]にある[[神社]]。'''陶器神社'''とも呼ばれる。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[天喜]]元年（[[1053年]]）、{{要出典範囲|仏説による[[末法思想|末法末世]]の世の1年目にあたることから|date=2010年5月}}、[[後冷泉天皇]]の勅願により[[源頼義]]が左女牛（さめがい）[[西洞院通|西洞院]]にあった自邸内の[[坤]]の隅に[[石清水八幡宮]]の若宮（[[新宮]]）を造営した。これが当社の始まりである。'''左女牛八幡宮'''と称され、また左女牛通の北を六条大路が走り、頼義邸はその間に位置したために'''六条左女牛若宮'''や'''六条八幡宮'''とも称された。 毎年8月に[[放生会]]を行う例とされたが、その後[[保延]]6年（[[1140年]]）1月に石清水八幡宮が火災によって社殿焼失に遭った際には左女牛の若宮に遷座したとの夢告ありと、京中の道俗男女の参詣が盛んとなったという『神道大辞典』。。爾来[[源氏]]の[[氏神]]として同氏からの崇敬を受け、[[源頼朝]]は[[大江広元]]の弟の[[季厳]]阿闍梨を[[別当|別当職]]に任じ、[[文治]]元年（[[1186年]]）12月には[[土佐国]][[吾川郡]]の地を寄進『[[吾妻鏡]]』同年12月30日条。している。翌年[[鎌倉幕府]]によって大規模な社殿の造営を行い、8月に遷宮している海老名尚、福田豊彦『六条八幡宮造営注文について』「国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告」p.p.350、1992。 頼朝は[[建久]]元年（[[1190年]]）と建久6年（[[1195年]]）に上洛した際、石清水八幡宮と当社の2社しか社参を行わなかった『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久元年11月11日条、同6年3月6日条。。以後[[鎌倉時代]]を通して{{要出典範囲|将軍家代々の社参が恒例とされたり|date=2010年5月}}、武家尊崇の大社として地方からも篤い崇敬を受けた。 [[承元]]2年（[[1208年]]）4月、付近一帯の大規模な火災『吾妻鏡』同年四月二五日条によって焼失するが、翌承元3年（[[1209年]]）12月に再建が完了し遷宮した海老名尚、福田豊彦『六条八幡宮造営注文について』「国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告」p.p.351、1992。[[嘉禎]]元年（[[1235年]]）12月には[[征夷大将軍|将軍]][[藤原頼経]]の病気平癒の祈祷の使者が[[執権]][[北条泰時]]より遣わされている『吾妻鏡』嘉禎元年12月24日条。 [[文永]]11年（[[1274年]]）に再び焼失する『続史愚抄』同年七月十四日条が、翌[[建治]]元年（[[1275年]]）、鎌倉に「造営用途支配状」（幕府に対する造営費用：6,734貫文）が届けられている海老名尚、福田豊彦『六条八幡宮造営注文について』「国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告」p.p.352、1992。 [[室町時代]]になっても{{要出典範囲|[[足利尊氏]]は7種の神宝や社領知行安堵状、[[足利義政|義政]]は51ヶ所の地所を奉納したり|date=2010年5月}}、[[足利将軍家]]から[[三条坊門殿|三条坊門]]の[[御所八幡宮]]と並んで代々の社参が行われるなど石清水八幡宮に次ぐ[[室町幕府]]の宗祀と崇められ、その結構は京中では[[八坂神社|祇園社]]、[[北野天満宮|北野社]]に次ぐ規模であった。しかし、[[応仁の乱]]の兵火に罹って社殿を焼失するなどし、幕府の権勢が弱まるのに合わせて衰微し、荒廃してしまった。のちには[[足利義輝]]及び[[足利義昭|義昭]]の発起により諸国の武家に寄付を募って再建がなされたものの、昔日の規模には至らなかった。 [[大内義隆]]、[[毛利氏]]・[[吉川氏]]・[[小早川氏]]三家、[[細川氏]]、[[三好氏]]、[[朝倉氏]]、[[織田信長]]などにより神地、神馬、太刀等が奉納されている。 [[天正]]12年（[[1584年]]）、[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による京都改造により[[東山 (京都府)|東山]]にあった[[御旅所]]に移され、旧地は[[本願寺]]（現・[[西本願寺]]）の寺域とされ、天正16年（[[1588年]]）には[[方広寺]]大仏殿（[[京の大仏]]）の北に、秀吉薨後の[[慶長]]10年（[[1605年]]）には現社地に三遷された。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1615年]]）、[[徳川家康]]により神領73石8斗余が寄進されている。 元和3年（[[1617年]]）に[[後水尾天皇]]の勅命で社殿が造営された。続いて、[[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）にも[[後光明天皇]]が父後水尾上皇の考えにより現在の本殿を造営している。その後、[[徳川将軍家]]より修理・神饌用として26石余が寄進された。 [[1877年]]（[[明治]]10年）、[[宮内省]]によって[[伏見街道]]十六町（現・東山区本町十六丁目）の円丘上にあった[[廃帝社|塚本社]]から[[仲恭天皇]]の神霊が奉遷され、合祀された塚本社のあった円丘は仲恭天皇の[[御陵]]であると伝えられ、塚本社は廃祠となったが、円丘は同天皇の[[陵墓参考地]]とされている（東山本町陵墓参考地）。。 [[1949年]]（[[昭和]]24年）には、当社が陶器の町の五条坂の中心にあることもあり、陶祖神である椎根津彦命が合祀された。このことから当社は「陶器神社」とも呼ばれるようになり、また、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）には「清水焼発祥之地 五条坂」の記念碑が建立された。 戦後は[[神社本教]]に参加し、現在、当社にその本部が置かれている。 [[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）12月1日には、日本初となる[[鍾馗]]を祀った神社である鍾馗神社が建立された。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[応神天皇]]、[[仲哀天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]（[[八幡三神]]） * 相殿神 - [[仲恭天皇]]（左相殿）、[[椎根津彦命|椎根津彦大神]]（陶祖神、右相殿） == 祭祀 == 三度の移転により[[氏子]]地域を有さない神社となっている。 === 年中行事 === * 1月元旦：元旦祭 * 1月3日：家運隆盛、商売繁盛祈願祭 * 1月7日：七種粥神事 * 1月15日：成人祭、新年祭 * 2月3日（又は4日）：節分祭（本社）、初午祭（末社稲荷社） * 2月15日：初卯祭 * 3月17日 - 21日：春の感謝祭 * 4月29日：天長節祭 * 5月4日：菖蒲祭 * 6月30日：[[大祓]]式 * 8月7日 - 10日：若宮八幡宮大祭、陶器神社大祭（陶器まつり） * 8月7日：仲恭天皇・[[孝明天皇]]祭 * 8月8日：仲哀天皇・神功皇后・応神天皇祭（若宮八幡宮例祭） * 8月9日：陶器神社祭 - [[清水焼]]の陶器市に併せて斎行 * 9月秋分の日：秋季祭 * 11月1日：弁財天大神火焚祭 * 11月8日：（本社）末社稲荷社火焚祭、末社秋葉神社火焚祭 * 11月15日：七五三詣 * 11月23日：新嘗祭、火焚祭 * 12月31日：大祓式、除夜祭 * 毎月1日・15日：月次祭 == 境内 == * 本殿（京都市指定有形[[文化財]]） - [[承応]]3年（[[1654年]]）再建。三間社[[流造]]。[[身舎]]正面の庇部分を前室とし、更にその前に[[向拝]]（こうはい）を付けた前室付流造という[[滋賀県]]には多いが京都では珍しい形式を持っている。屋根は当初は[[檜皮葺]]であった京都市文化財保護課、「[http://www.city.kyoto.jp/bunshi/bunkazai/siteisyasinn/kennsetumei02.htm#Anchor-36697 京都市指定・登録文化財－建造物]」（平成22年5月31日閲覧）。が、現在は銅板葺。[[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）6月1日に京都市指定有形文化財（建造物）に指定された。 * 幣殿 * 拝殿 * 醒ケ井文庫 * 神饌所 * [[神社本教]]本部 * 庭園 * 放生池 * 手水鉢 - [[足利義満]]が奉納した[[至徳 (日本)|至徳]]3年（[[1386年]]）銘の八角形の手水鉢。 * [[孝明天皇]]御胞衣埋納所 * [[中御門天皇]]皇女六宮御胞衣埋納所 * 慶光天皇（[[閑院宮典仁親王]]）皇子良宮御胞衣埋納所 * 社務所 * 清水焼発祥之地 五条坂の碑 - [[1985年]]（昭和60年）造。 == 摂・末社 == * 阿波[[天満宮]] - 祭神：[[菅原道真公]]。創建は[[天喜]]元年（[[1053年]]）12月15日。もとは南向天満宮と呼ばれていたが、[[阿波国]][[徳島城]]主の[[蜂須賀家政]]とその子孫が篤く信仰していたことにより、阿波天満宮と呼ばれるようになった。若宮天満宮ともいい、[[洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]]第19番札所。 * [[秋葉神社]] - 祭神：[[火之夜藝速男神|火乃焼速尾命（ひのやぎはやおのみこと）]] * [[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[宇賀御魂命]] * 祖霊社 陶祖神社 * 竹生島神社 - 祭神：[[市杵島姫命]] * 産霊神社 - 祭神：[[高皇産霊大神]] * 蛭子社 - 祭神：[[蛭子神|蛭子之神]] * 鍾馗神社 - 祭神：[[鍾馗]]。[[2013年]]（[[平成]]25年）12月1日建立。日本初となる鍾馗を祀った神社である。 == 文化財 == === 京都市指定有形文化財 === * 本殿 附：棟札 2枚 * 若宮八幡宮社関係資料108点 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[明治]]にかけての資料で、中世を通じて歴代足利将軍に篤く保護されたことを証するものでもあり、特に「足利将軍参詣絵巻」は将軍の社参の絵画資料として貴重である京都市文化財保護課、「[http://www.city.kyoto.jp/bunshi/bunkazai/siteisyasinn/bikousetumei02.html#Anchor-47318 京都市指定・登録文化財－美術工芸品]」（平成22年5月31日閲覧）。。[[平成]]16年（[[2004年]]）4月1日に京都市の文化財（美術工芸品）に指定された。 == 前後の札所 == ; [[洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]] : 18 [[高台寺|高台寺天満宮]]　-　'''19 若宮天満宮'''　-　20 [[萬年寺|紅梅天満宮]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『神道大辞典』（平凡社、昭和15年刊の縮刷復刻版）、臨川書店、昭和44年ISBN 4-653-01347-0 == 外部リンク == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp 若宮八幡宮社公式サイト] * [http://www.kyototsuu.jp/Jinjya/WakamiyaHachimanGuu.html 若宮八幡宮社]（京都通百科事典） * [http://www.toukimaturi.gr.jp/ 京都・五条坂 –陶器まつり–]（陶器祭運営協議会） * {{Wayback|url=http://alay.at.webry.info/200610/article_16.html |title=若宮八幡宮（京都市東山区） – あらいのじかん |date=20090131170443}}（個人ブログ） * [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:東山区の建築物]] [[Category:東山区の歴史]] [[Category:洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]] [[Category:源頼義]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]]

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: * Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053年}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584年}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605年}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654年}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year }}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year }}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584th year }}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605th year }}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654th year }}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year }}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year }}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584th year }}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605th year }}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654th year }}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:Former gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto City shrines]] [[Category:Higashiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Sources cited]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区|12=draft}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year|12=draft}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|12=draft}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year|12=draft}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義|12=draft}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|12=draft}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584th year|12=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605th year|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|12=draft}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇|12=draft}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=draft}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|12=draft}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654th year|12=draft}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|12=draft}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区|12=draft|12=draft}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|12=draft|12=draft}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|ja|東山区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|ja|天喜|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1053|ja|1053rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|ja|後冷泉天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源頼義|12=draft|12=draft}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|12=draft|12=draft}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|ja|源氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|ja|1584th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashiyama, Kyoto|ja|東山|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|ja|1605th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|ja|仲恭天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|ja|1654th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|12=draft|12=draft}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==
