In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū
revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kōchi)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kōchi)|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

* {{ill|Kawakami-yama Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|川上山若宮八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami-yama Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kōchi)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kōchi)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Daijingū|ja|若宮八幡大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Daijingū|qq=}}

* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku)|ja|若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Tōkaichi)|ja|若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Tōkaichi)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū Shrine (Kyoto)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū Shrine (Kyoto)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|若宮八幡宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Shrine | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya6044.jpg|250px|Wakamiya Hachimangū]]
Pathway | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)|lt=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kōchi)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Kōchi)|qq=}}

[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|qid=Q1203862}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616253]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|qid=Q1203862}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616253]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|qid=Q1203862}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616253]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]] Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|qq=|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|qid=Q1203862}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Tengi|Tengi]] 1 ([[:en:1053|1053]]) by the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Reizei|Emperor Go-Reizei]]. It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

It was originally constructed by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|qq=|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] tutelary shrine of the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto clan]]. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|qq=|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], and gradually declined in influence. In [[:en:1584|1584]], the shrine was relocated to [[:en:東山|Higashiyama, Kyoto]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], eventually settling at its current site in [[:en:1605|1605]]. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: [[:en:Emperor Chūkyō|Emperor Chūkyō]] (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in [[:en:1654|1654]], featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==
