{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|en|Emperor Go-Reizei|WD=Q356060|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|東山|en|東山|WD=Q17055915|lt=Higashiyama, Kyoto|1=東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605|1=1605}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|en|Emperor Chūkyō|WD=Q357253|lt=Emperor Chūkyō|1=Emperor Chūkyō}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

[[co:commons:Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kyoto)]][[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[co:commons:Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kyoto)]][[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kyoto)]][[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|en|Emperor Go-Reizei|WD=Q356060|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|東山|en|東山|WD=Q17055915|lt=Higashiyama, Kyoto|1=東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605|1=1605}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|en|Emperor Chūkyō|WD=Q357253|lt=Emperor Chūkyō|1=Emperor Chūkyō}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==

[[co:commons:Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kyoto)]][[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[co:commons:Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kyoto)]][[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|en|Emperor Go-Reizei|WD=Q356060|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|東山|en|東山|WD=Q17055915|lt=Higashiyama, Kyoto|1=東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605|1=1605}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|en|Emperor Chūkyō|WD=Q357253|lt=Emperor Chūkyō|1=Emperor Chūkyō}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==
* {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Hitachiota)|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Hitachiota)|1=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616252}}

'''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' {{otheruses|A shrine in Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|general explanation of Wakamiya Hachimangū|Wakamiya Hachimangū}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} Gojo Hashihigashi 5-chome 480 | Latitude = 34 | LatMin = 59 | LatSec = 43.4 | N_or_S = N | Longitude = 135 | LongMin = 46 | LongSec = 29.6 | E_or_W = E | MapCountry = JP-26 | Map = Japan Kyoto city2#Japan Kyoto#Japan | LabelPosition = left | EnshrinedDeities = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) | MainHall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing | Alternatenames = Rokujō Samegai Hachimangū・Tōki Shrine | Pilgrimage = {{ill|Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|ja|洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakuyō Tenmangū 25 Shrine Pilgrimage|1=The 25 Shrines of Rakuyo Tenmangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18054963}} 19th station (Wakamiya Tenmangū) | AnnualFestivals = August 8 | Events = | location = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|WD=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.995389|N|135.774889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 清水焼発祥之地 石碑.jpg|thumb|180px|Monument: The Origin of Kiyomizu Pottery]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 鍾馗.jpg|thumb|180px|Shōki Shrine and its statue]] [[File:若宮八幡宮 - 本殿2.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū Shrine''' (若宮八幡宮社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. It is also known as the "Tōki Shrine." Its former Rank under the pre-war shrine system was {{ill|Gōsha (Shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tengi|1=Tengi}} 1 ({{ill|1053|en|1053|WD=Q19368|lt=1053|1=1053}}) by the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|en|Emperor Go-Reizei|WD=Q356060|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}. It was originally constructed by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} in the southwest corner of his residence located along {{ill|Nishinotōin Street|ja|西洞院通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Street|1=Nishinotōin Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629029}} in Samegai (さめがい). Initially named "Samegai Hachimangū" or "Rokujō Samegai Wakamiya," the shrine received widespread reverence as the {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} tutelary shrine of the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}}. For centuries, the shrine enjoyed support from both imperial and warrior families. It was visited by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} during his travels to Kyoto, and later by the {{ill|Ashikaga shogunate|ja|足利将軍家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogun family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28692536}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, becoming one of the most prominent shrines in Kyoto. However, it faced repeated destruction by fire, including during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, and gradually declined in influence. In {{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584|1=1584}}, the shrine was relocated to {{ill|東山|en|東山|WD=Q17055915|lt=Higashiyama, Kyoto|1=東山}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, eventually settling at its current site in {{ill|1605|en|1605|WD=Q6741|lt=1605|1=1605}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Principal deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (the {{ill|Three Deities of Hachiman|ja|八幡三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}) * Associate deities: {{ill|Emperor Chūkyō|en|Emperor Chūkyō|WD=Q357253|lt=Emperor Chūkyō|1=Emperor Chūkyō}} (Left shrine), {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}} (Right shrine, God of Pottery) == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja|京都市指定有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ** Main Hall: Rebuilt in {{ill|1654|en|1654|WD=Q6970|lt=1654|1=1654}}, featuring a unique Nagare-zukuri style rarely seen in Kyoto. == festivals == * January 1: New Year's Festival * August 8: {{ill|Hachiman Matsuri|ja|八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 9: Tōki Shrine Festival (aligned with the Kiyomizu Pottery Market) == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-hachimangu.jp Official Site] == All the ones that actually exist ==
